raw.objective: “To establish a regression equation to properly estimate the unbound serum concentration of valproic acid (VPA) from its total serum concentration; the relationship between total and unbound serum VPA concentrations was retrospectively characterized.”
raw.observations: Comedication (ZON, PHT, CBZ) are reported as having no effect on VPA CL. Ethosuximine affects relationship between total and unbound VPA concentrations, causing a decrease with comedication. A decrease in [VPA] with comedication carbamazepine, may be due to increased distribution of VPA in erythrocytes.
raw.dose: Steady state, oral doses in patients
raw.structure: Langmuir model (relating free concentration to total concentration)
raw.equations: Ct=Cf+Bm*(Cf/(Kd+Cf)) [monotherapy] Kd=theta1 (μg/mL) *(1+eta1) Bm= theta2 *(1+eta2) [combination (VPA w/ ZON, ETH, PHT, CPZ, CBZ, CLO)] Kd=theta3 (μg/mL) *(1+eta3) Bm= theta4 *(1+eta4) [polytherapy] Kd=theta5(μg/mL) *(1+eta5) Bm= theta6 *(1+eta6) Ct: total VPA concentration Cf: free VPA concentration
raw.parameter_values: theta1=7.8 theta2= 130 theta3=6.8 theta4=130 theta5=4.2 theta6=105.8 omega1=0.0080 omega2=0.0012 omega3=0.0078 omega4=0.0012 omega5=0.0091 omega6=0.0012

study.dose: Steady state, oral doses in patients
study.objective: “To establish a regression equation to properly estimate the unbound serum concentration of valproic acid (VPA) from its total serum concentration; the relationship between total and unbound serum VPA concentrations was retrospectively characterized.”

normalization.omega: Values labelled OMEGA in the appendix are retained as NONMEM variances; no CV reconversion is applied.
normalization.eta: ETA distribution is inferred only when the appendix equation explicitly uses exp(ETA) or additive ETA.
normalization.source_text: The appendix row is preserved verbatim in raw; normalized fields never replace it.
